name: Fresh Raspberry Tamales with Ginger Custard
recipeInstructions:
In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle, beat the butter until light and fluffy (4-5 minutes). Add the masa, baking powder, salt, and sugar and mix until blended. Add the boiling water, corn puree, raspberry puree, and vanilla and mix just until combined. The dough should be light and very moist. Spread the dough on soaked corn husks in a square about 1/4 inch thick. Distribute some of the raisins, almonds, and candied lemon peel in the center and roll up to form a cylindrical shape. Tie or fold the ends of the husks over to enclose the tamale. Steam tamales for 30 minutes and serve warm with some of the ginger custard sauce and a garnish of fresh berries and mint sprigs.
Yield: 10-12 tamales
